Title: Down-Regulation of Cytokinin Receptor Gene SlHK2 Improves Plant Tolerance to Drought, Heat, and Combined Stresses in Tomato

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: SlHK2 is referred to as tomato histidine kinase 2, a cytokinin receptor homologous to Arabidopsis AHK2. SlHK2 RNAi plants display increased root length under normal conditions and significantly higher cuticular wax loads than wild type. Abiotic stresses down-regulate SlHK2 and cytokinin response regulator genes (SlRR1, SlRR3, SlRR5), consistent with a role of reduced cytokinin signaling in acclimation to drought and heat. The improved stomatal conductance under stress in SlHK2 RNAi plants is proposed to be partly related to their enhanced root system.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SlHK2 is a tomato cytokinin receptor histidine kinase whose expression is suppressed by abiotic stress and whose knockdown via RNAi (SlHK2 RNAi lines L1 and L2) significantly reduces SlHK2 transcript levels.
2. SlHK2 RNAi plants show enhanced tolerance to drought, heat, and combined drought + heat stresses, with less wilting, higher relative water content, and lower electrolyte leakage than wild type.
3. Under stress, SlHK2 RNAi plants maintain higher photosynthetic rate, stomatal conductance, PSII efficiency (Fq'/Fm', ETR, qL, Fv/Fm), and higher expression of PSII oxygen-evolving complex genes PsbP and PsbQ compared with wild type.
4. SlHK2 RNAi plants accumulate less MDA and H2O2 and display weaker DAB staining under stress, concomitant with higher activities and transcript levels of antioxidant enzymes (GR, APX, SOD, POD, DHAR, CAT).
5. SlHK2 RNAi plants accumulate more osmoprotectants (proline, glycine betaine, trehalose, soluble sugars) and show up-regulation of osmoprotectant synthesis genes (SlSPS, SlP5CS, SlT6PS) under drought, heat, and combined stresses.
6. Cuticular wax biosynthesis genes (SlCER3, SlLTP1, SlTTS1, SlKCS1) are up-regulated and total leaf cuticular wax loads are higher in SlHK2 RNAi plants, even under control conditions, correlating with reduced electrolyte leakage.
7. Stress- and ABA-related genes SIDREB1 and SINCED, as well as heat shock genes SlHSP17.4 and SlHSP21, are more strongly induced in SlHK2 RNAi plants than in wild type under relevant stress conditions.
8. Overall, SlHK2 functions as a negative regulator of drought, heat, and combined stress tolerance in tomato, and its down-regulation improves multi-stress resilience.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi-mediated knockdown of the cytokinin receptor SlHK2 acts as a loss-of-function that increases tomato tolerance to drought, heat, and combined drought + heat stresses, characterized by reduced leaf wilting, higher relative water content, lower electrolyte leakage and oxidative damage, better maintenance of photosynthetic performance, stronger antioxidant defense, greater osmoprotectant accumulation, and increased cuticular wax deposition compared with wild type.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: SlHK2 encodes a cytokinin receptor histidine kinase that functions as a negative regulator of drought, heat, and combined stress tolerance. Its down-regulation suppresses cytokinin signaling, likely increases ABA sensitivity, and is associated with up-regulation of drought-responsive (SIDREB1, SINCED), heat shock, antioxidant, osmoprotectant synthesis, and cuticular wax biosynthesis genes, collectively enhancing water retention, membrane stability, ROS scavenging, osmotic adjustment, and cuticular barrier formation under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Cytokinin signaling pathway in tomato, where SlHK2 (a cytokinin receptor) mediates cytokinin responses and negatively modulates abiotic stress adaptation, interacting with ABA-related drought signaling, antioxidant defense pathways, osmoprotectant synthesis pathways, and cuticular wax biosynthesis under drought, heat, and combined stresses.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Down-regulation of the cytokinin receptor SlHK2 (via RNAi or stress-induced suppression) is associated with increased expression of multiple downstream stress-responsive genes, including drought/ABA-related genes SIDREB1 and SINCED, heat-shock genes SlHSP17.4 and SlHSP21, cuticular wax biosynthesis genes SlCER3, SlLTP1, SlTTS1, and SlKCS1, antioxidant genes SlGR1, SlcAPX, SlSOD, and SlCAT1, and osmoprotectant synthesis genes SlSPS, SlP5CS, and SlT6PS under drought, heat, and combined stresses, indicating that SlHK2 negatively regulates these stress-adaptive pathways.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Histidine kinase-type cytokinin receptor (tomato histidine kinase 2)
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: SlHK2 expression is reduced in SlHK2 RNAi lines L1 and L2 relative to wild type, and abiotic stress treatments (drought, heat, combined) suppress SlHK2 expression; a GUS reporter driven by the 35S promoter in the SlHK2 RNAi construct is active in multiple tissues (leaf, fruit, sepal), with activity patterns depending on developmental stage.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Four treatments applied to 35-day-old plants for 5 consecutive days: (1) well-watered control, 26/20 °C day/night with daily watering; (2) drought stress, 26/20 °C day/night with no watering; (3) heat stress, 32/26 °C day/night with daily watering; and (4) combined drought + heat stress, 32/26 °C day/night with no watering.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought, heat, and combined drought + heat), cytokinin signaling, photosynthetic performance, antioxidant defense, osmoprotectant metabolism, and cuticular wax biosynthesis in tomato.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought stress, heat stress, and combined drought + heat stress), including maintenance of photosynthetic performance and oxidative stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Compared with wild type under drought, heat, and combined stresses, SlHK2 RNAi plants show: significantly higher relative water content and lower electrolyte leakage; markedly smaller reductions in photosynthetic rate, stomatal conductance, Fq'/Fm', ETR, qL, and Fv/Fm; significantly lower MDA and H2O2 contents and weaker DAB staining; higher activities of GR, APX, SOD, POD, DHAR, and CAT; higher contents of osmoprotectants (proline, GB, trehalose, sucrose, glucose, fructose) with smaller decreases in starch; 1.5-fold higher expression of drought/ABA-related genes SIDREB1 and SINCED under drought and combined stress; strong up-regulation of heat-shock genes SlHSP17.4 and SlHSP21 under heat and combined stresses; higher expression of wax biosynthesis genes (SlCER3, SlLTP1, SlTTS1, SlKCS1) and increased total wax loads; and higher expression of antioxidant (SlGR1, SlcAPX, SlSOD, SlCAT1) and osmoprotectant synthesis genes (SlSPS, SlP5CS, SlT6PS), especially under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under non-stress conditions, SlHK2 RNAi plants exhibit increased root length and higher total cuticular wax loads compared with wild type. Under drought, heat, and combined stresses, they show reduced MDA and H2O2 accumulation, enhanced activities of GR, APX, SOD, POD, DHAR, and CAT, and higher accumulation of osmoprotectants (proline, glycine betaine, trehalose, sucrose, glucose, fructose) with relatively smaller reductions in starch content than wild type.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tomato (Solanum lycopersicon) cv. Micro-Tom wild-type plants and independent homozygous SlHK2 RNAi transgenic lines (T3 generation; lines L1 and L2) carrying a p35S:SlHK2-RNAi construct with a GUS reporter.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Design of a 234-bp SlHK2 CDS fragment for RNAi, cloning into the 2300GNR vector under a CaMV35S promoter (p35S:SlHK2-RNAi) and transformation of tomato cv. Micro-Tom; selection of T3 homozygous RNAi lines; imposition of controlled drought, heat, and combined stresses in growth chambers; measurement of relative water content and electrolyte leakage; gas exchange and chlorophyll fluorescence measurements; quantification of osmoprotectants (proline, glycine betaine, soluble sugars, trehalose, starch); antioxidant enzyme activity assays (GR, APX, SOD, POD, DHAR, CAT); H2O2 content and DAB staining; cuticular wax extraction and quantification by GC-MS/GC-FID; and qRT-PCR analysis of SlHK2 and multiple stress-, antioxidant-, osmoprotectant-, photosystem-, and wax-related genes using SlActin as reference.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic RNAi knockdown lines (SlHK2 RNAi, lines L1 and L2) generated in tomato cv. Micro-Tom using a 35S-driven SlHK2 RNAi construct were phenotypically and physiologically analyzed under controlled drought, heat, and combined stress treatments, together with qRT-PCR confirmation of SlHK2 silencing and downstream gene-expression changes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse-genetic approach using an SlHK2 coding-sequence fragment cloned into a CaMV35S-driven RNAi construct (p35S:SlHK2-RNAi) to silence SlHK2 in tomato, followed by selection of homozygous RNAi lines for functional characterization.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Down-regulation of the cytokinin receptor gene SlHK2 via RNAi enhances tolerance of tomato to drought, heat, and combined drought + heat stresses by improving water status, maintaining membrane integrity, protecting photosynthetic machinery, increasing antioxidant enzyme capacity, promoting osmoprotectant accumulation, and elevating cuticular wax loads. These results suggest that SlHK2 is a promising negative regulator target for breeding or engineering tomato and possibly other crops with improved resilience to increasingly frequent combined abiotic stresses under climate change.
